item.title: The Buyers Behind Gold’s $5,000 Breakthrough
item.description: <![CDATA[Businessweek Finance The Buyers Behind Gold’s $5,000 Breakthrough No longer a “hide under your mattress” asset, the precious metal is enticing a whole new crop of global traders. Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Yvonne Yue Li and Ella Feldman February 27, 2026 at 10:00 AM UTC Bookmark Save Obioha Okereke used to think investing in gold was for risk-averse retirees. “I felt it was boring, to put it bluntly,” says the technology consultant, 29, who’s been meticulously managing his ambitious, growth-focused portfolio since his senior year of high school. But as his tech-heavy investments started to wobble last year and the metal extended its climb, Okereke decided to give gold a go. He bought into his first gold exchange-traded fund in September, first with $100, adding over $2,000 more since. It’s a small share of his overall holdings, but one he intends to keep increasing: His gold investment is up nearly 17% since he started, outperforming the rest of his portfolio. “I wanted something that was going to be a hedge against the uncertainty and the random swings,” Okereke says. “Gold just seemed to make sense.”]]>

item.title: European Stocks Eye Longest Monthly Winning Streak Since 2013
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets European Stocks Eye Longest Monthly Winning Streak Since 2013 Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Macarena Munoz Montijano and Rose Henderson February 27, 2026 at 8:24 AM UTC Updated on February 27, 2026 at 9:54 AM UTC Bookmark Save European shares rose in February’s final trading session as they headed for the longest monthly winning streak since 2013, with the region increasingly popular as investors seek alternatives to the US. The Stoxx Europe 600 Index was 0.3% higher by 9:52 a.m. in London and trading at a fresh record high. It’s up 4% in February and headed for an eighth month of gains. Telecom and miners fared best in Friday’s session, while the travel sector was the biggest laggard.]]>

item.title: S&P 500 Faces February Loss as Europe, Asia Rally: Markets Wrap
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets S&P 500 Faces February Loss as Europe, Asia Rally: Markets Wrap Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Andre Janse Van Vuuren and Macarena Munoz Montijano February 26, 2026 at 10:31 PM UTC Updated on February 27, 2026 at 9:53 AM UTC Bookmark Save The S&P 500 is set for a monthly loss after a whirlwind February marked by twin fears of a bubble in the artificial-intelligence trade and of the technology’s disruptive power. Futures on the US benchmark were 0.3% lower after Thursday’s selloff in chipmakers erased gains for the week. Europe’s Stoxx 600 was on track for an eighth straight monthly advance, its longest such streak in more than a decade. MSCI Inc.’s Asia-Pacific index headed for its best February on record.]]>

item.title: UK’s February Gas Exports to EU Hit Three-Year Seasonal High
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets UK’s February Gas Exports to EU Hit Three-Year Seasonal High Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Elena Mazneva February 27, 2026 at 9:44 AM UTC Bookmark Save Natural gas exports from the UK to continental Europe in February rose to the highest level for this time of year since 2023, as the country benefited from ample supply and milder weather. Britain has sent more than 400 million cubic meters of the fuel through a key pipeline connecting it with Belgium so far this month, a shift from net imports last year, grid data compiled by Bloomberg show.]]>

item.title: Tusk Says EU Loans Won’t Impact Polish Defense Deals With US
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets Tusk Says EU Loans Won’t Impact Polish Defense Deals With US Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Zosia Wanat February 27, 2026 at 9:41 AM UTC Bookmark Save The European Union’s defense loan program won’t affect Polish purchases of US weapons or cooperation between Warsaw and Washington, Prime Minister Donald Tusk said on Friday. Poland is the largest beneficiary of the EU’s funding, known as SAFE, with nearly €44 billion ($52 billion) in earmarked funding. But its nationalist President Karol Nawrocki , who seeks closer ties with the US and is in opposition to the pro-EU Tusk, may veto legislation enabling the country to more easily tap the loans.]]>

item.title: China Halts Canada Canola Meal Tariffs, Adding to Trade Thaw
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets China Halts Canada Canola Meal Tariffs, Adding to Trade Thaw Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Megan Durisin Albery and Josh Xiao February 27, 2026 at 9:41 AM UTC Bookmark Save China said it would drop tariffs on Canadian rapeseed meal and lobsters, easing disruptions to agricultural trade after Prime Minister Mark Carney’s January visit. The halt — which also applies to peas and crabs — will take effect from March 1 to Dec. 31, the government said Bloomberg Terminal Friday. It comes after Canada pledged to significantly cut import duties on 49,000 Chinese electric vehicles, helping to repair a trade rift that has tested relations between the nations.]]>

item.title: India in Talks With US as Court Overturns Sweeping Trump Tariffs
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets India in Talks With US as Court Overturns Sweeping Trump Tariffs Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Shruti Srivastava February 27, 2026 at 9:40 AM UTC Bookmark Save India is in discussions with the US on the evolving tariff situation and will ensure that its interests are protected under the recently concluded trade deal, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said on Friday. Goyal’s remarks at an event in New Delhi came days after the US Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump ’s sweeping global tariffs, creating market uncertainty. Before the ruling, India and the US announced an interim trade agreement that lowered tariffs on Indian goods to 18% from 50%, ending long negotiations.]]>

item.title: New World Development Posts First-Half Loss as Woes Persist
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets New World Development Posts First-Half Loss as Woes Persist Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Expand New World Development Co. is one of Hong Kong’s Big Four developers. Photographer: Lam Yik/Bloomberg Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Shawna Kwan February 27, 2026 at 8:37 AM UTC Updated on February 27, 2026 at 9:38 AM UTC Bookmark Save New World Development Co. posted a loss in the first half as Hong Kong’s property downturn continued to weigh on the distressed developer. The company lost HK$3.73 billion ($477 million) in the six months ended Dec. 31, according to a filing to the Hong Kong stock exchange Friday. That narrowed from a HK$6.6 billion loss a year earlier. New World’s business year ends in June.]]>

item.title: Wizz Air Drops After Anchor Investor Sells 10% Stake in Carrier
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets Wizz Air Drops After Anchor Investor Sells 10% Stake in Carrier Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Kate Duffy February 27, 2026 at 9:33 AM UTC Bookmark Save Wizz Air Holdings Plc stock fell the most since July after its key shareholder sold a 10% stake in the budget carrier. Shares dropped as much as 10% Friday after Indigo Partners LLC ’s investment funds offered about 10 million shares in Wizz priced at £12.50 ($16.87), equating to a 9.7% stake, according to a filing.]]>

item.title: Hungary, Slovakia Demand Ukraine Let In Pipeline Inspection Team
item.description: <![CDATA[Markets Hungary, Slovakia Demand Ukraine Let In Pipeline Inspection Team Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift Gift this article Contact us: Provide news feedback or report an error Confidential tip? Send a tip to our reporters Site feedback: Take our Survey New Window Facebook X LinkedIn Email Link Gift By Thomas Escritt February 27, 2026 at 9:27 AM UTC Bookmark Save Hungary and Slovakia are seeking to send a team of inspectors to Ukraine to examine the condition of the Druzhba oil pipeline, Prime Minister Viktor Orban said after a phone call with his Slovak counterpart Robert Fico. Hungary and Slovakia haven’t received Russian oil via the pipeline since a late January Russian attack. Hungary and Slovakia allege that Ukraine isn’t letting through Russian oil due to a political decision, while Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy has maintained that the infrastructure was damaged and repairs need time.]]>
